extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won a reduced sprint after surviving final climb 14km out, navigating chaos when lead-outs collapsed before final roundabout.
tactical_patterns: Bora paced entire day despite having best sprinter, revealing confidence in Sagan's no-leadout positioning strategy over energy conservation.
INEOS used Kelderman and Camna to chase Cavagna despite no GC stakes, protecting against sprint crashes not GC threats.
Quick-Step overcommitted leadout riders early then abandoned Cavagna in last kilometer, exposing coordination breakdown in dual-objective races.
Sagan deliberately ignored Meeus's 300m explosion, waiting for top-five wheel positioning rather than following teammate's doomed acceleration.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: First bunch sprint win in two years despite disrupted COVID prep shows form peaks faster than traditional Grand Tour build
rider_signals.MOLANO Sebastian: negative: Had speed to beat Sagan but chose Soto's sixth-position wheel over following Sagan fourth-wheel, exposing positioning inexperience
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Remy: negative: 7-second maximum gap despite good form shows sprinter teams now instantly shut down any breakaway specialist regardless of climb difficulty
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: Pacing for Sagan sprint despite GC podium position reveals Bora prioritized stage wins over defending third place
rider_signals.KNOX James: neutral: INEOS allowed attack over final climb crest then immediately chased, suggesting they tested sprinter team responses not seeking result
